overview.description: AI-first marketing recommendations layer for Shopify e-commerce brands
overview.description_long: marsmars.ai builds an agentic AI recommendation system that plugs into Shopify, CRM/CDP, and Klaviyo to identify where revenue is leaking and assign clear, role-specific actions to each marketing team member. Targets DTC brands with $2M–$15M in annual revenue and 3–10 person marketing teams. Generates next-best-action recommendations for paid marketers, lifecycle teams, and CRO teams — delivering prioritized execution instead of dashboards.
overview.tagline: AI-first marketing intelligence layer for e-commerce brands that tells your marketing team exactly what to do next

history.origin_story: Founding team formed at Harvard Innovation Labs in Boston in August 2025. Customer discovery began June 2025. Jiri Nekovar (CEO) is a 2x founder and fractional CMO with e-commerce expertise; Dhinesh Thiru (COO) is a former Demand Planning Manager at Mejuri and Target.

key_milestones.event: Founded at Harvard Innovation Labs, Boston (August 2025)
Early access launched for 100+ e-commerce marketing teams (March 2026)

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: The core value proposition — recommending marketing actions from Shopify/CRM data — is easily replicable with general-purpose LLMs. No proprietary training data or technical moat at this stage; the product is a thin AI orchestration layer over existing data integrations.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: E-commerce marketing analytics is a real and growing market (DTC brands are a large segment), but it is heavily competed with established tools like Triple Whale, Northbeam, Klaviyo, and increasingly native AI from Shopify itself. Pre-PMF with no confirmed paying customers.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Pre-seed stage with no known funding raised, only 1-2 employees, and still in early access/beta as of March 2026. Actively fundraising but no runway signals beyond founder bootstrapping.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Very early-stage startup with high AGI displacement risk, no funding, and a competitive market dominated by well-resourced incumbents. The Czech founder connection and Harvard incubator origin provide some credibility, but the company has not yet demonstrated PMF or secured investment.

metadata.notes: Company has Prague addresses on CNAIP (Růžová 970/7 and Národní 135/14, Praha 1) despite being founded at Harvard Innovation Labs in Boston. LinkedIn shows Austin, TX as location. Founder Jiri Nekovar is Czech. The company email domain is marsmars.cz. Founded August 2025, extremely early stage with early access launched March 2026.
